title: ensureCiteContractPolicyActivatedMarker with bootstrap-drift gate
description: Add new marker emission function for cite_contract_policy_activated event. Unlike rc.20 cite_policy_activated marker, this one is gated on bootstrap-drift detection: if BOOTSTRAP drift is present, refuse to emit and return blocked_by='bootstrap_drift'. Caller (runDoctorCiteCoverage) renders 'contract_check: skipped (bootstrap drift — run fab install)' in report.

files.change: Add `async function ensureCiteContractPolicyActivatedMarker(projectRoot: string): Promise<{ marker_ts: number; emitted_now: boolean; blocked_by: 'bootstrap_drift' | null }>`. Implementation: (1) Call existing bootstrap-drift detection helper (locate via grep BOOTSTRAP_REGEX or check rc.23 self-heal logic); (2) If drift detected → return { marker_ts: 0, emitted_now: false, blocked_by: 'bootstrap_drift' }; (3) Else mirror ensureCitePolicyActivatedMarker exactly — readEventLedger filtering event_type='cite_contract_policy_activated', if no existing marker emit one via writeEventLedger, return { marker_ts, emitted_now, blocked_by: null }.

files.target: add new describe block for ensureCiteContractPolicyActivatedMarker
files.change: Test cases: (1) clean bootstrap + no prior marker → emits new marker with marker_emitted_now=true; (2) clean bootstrap + existing marker → returns existing marker_ts with emitted_now=false; (3) drifted bootstrap → returns blocked_by='bootstrap_drift', no emission; (4) idempotency: two consecutive calls emit only once; (5) corrupted ledger → graceful return matching rc.20 marker behavior. ≥5 cases.

implementation: Read packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts L5455-L5497 to confirm ensureCitePolicyActivatedMarker implementation pattern.
Locate existing bootstrap-drift detection helper. Likely candidates: detectBootstrapDrift in skills-and-hooks.ts, or doctor-side fault check. Grep for BOOTSTRAP_REGEX or BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL.

If no reusable helper exists, write minimal detector: read project's AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc, locate managed block via BOOTSTRAP_MARKER_BEGIN/END, byte-compare extracted content vs BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL.
Implement ensureCiteContractPolicyActivatedMarker calling drift detector first.
Add test cases per files[].change directive with mocked ledger + bootstrap fixtures.
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test doctor` to confirm green.

reference.examples: rc.20 TASK-04 introduced ensureCitePolicyActivatedMarker; rc.24 replicates with drift-gate addition.

rationale.chosen_approach: Drift-gated marker — refuses activation until tool chain is consistent. Zero false-violation risk during upgrade window.
alternatives_considered: Marker emit at install (rejected per grill-me B5-β analysis: changes marker ownership semantics)
Accept transition noise (rejected: violates B4-y first-day-clean design)
rationale.tradeoffs: User must complete `fab install` for contract policy to activate; documented in TASK-12 CHANGELOG migration section.

manual_checks: Manual: scaffold project with drifted BOOTSTRAP, run fab doctor --cite-coverage → confirm skipped:bootstrap_drift
Manual: clean bootstrap → marker emits on first run, no-op on second

criteria: Function returns object with marker_ts, emitted_now, blocked_by fields
Function calls bootstrap-drift detector before emit
On drift detected: returns blocked_by='bootstrap_drift' AND no ledger write
Commit msg: 'feat(rc24): cite_contract_policy_activated marker with drift gate (TASK-06)'
